headline: Klang Valley Commuter Rail Integration Plan Announced
context: Transport Ministry announced RM4.8 billion plan to integrate all Klang Valley rail systems under single ticketing and scheduling framework.

cards.big: RM4.8 billion rail integration plan for Klang Valley announced
cards.sub: Single ticketing and scheduling would unify MRT LRT KTM and Monorail systems

cards.big: Plan eliminates transfer penalties saving average commuter RM4.50 per journey
cards.sub: Cross-platform transfers at 18 interchange stations would be seamless by 2029

cards.big: Daily rail ridership projected to increase 35% from 1.2 million to 1.62 million
cards.sub: Revenue sharing formula between operators remains primary unresolved dispute

cards.big: Prasarana KTM and MRT Corp would retain separate operations under unified fare
cards.sub: Similar integration in Hong Kong took 8 years and multiple failed attempts

cards.big: Rail integration tests whether institutional interests can align for commuter benefit
Klang Valley rail integration succeeds only if fare structures and timetables align across operators.
